entertainer_information.music_background.answer:
Canal City Acoustic Duo is a Welland-based duo made up of Emily Carter (vocals, acoustic guitar) and Daniel Nguyen (acoustic guitar, backing vocals, light percussion). We focus on stripped-down, harmony-driven versions of familiar songs that work well in a family-friendly market environment. 

Emily grew up in St. Catharines and has been singing in choirs and small bands since high school. She studied Music (vocal performance) part-time at Brock University and has performed at a variety of community events including the Niagara Grape & Wine Festival side stages, local coffeehouses (Black Sheep Lounge, The Grounds Coffeehouse), and several charity fundraisers for the Niagara SPCA. Emily’s style leans toward folk, light pop, and singer-songwriter material, and she often rearranges more upbeat songs into mellow, acoustic versions suitable for morning audiences. 

Daniel started on classical guitar as a teenager and later branched into fingerstyle and acoustic pop. He has played in church bands and small jazz combos around the Niagara region and has done session work for a couple of local independent artists, contributing both guitar and basic home recording/production. He handles most of the arranging for the duo, building medleys and transitions so that sets flow smoothly without long pauses between songs. 

As a duo, we’ve developed a rotating repertoire of about 3.5 hours of material, which allows us to tailor each 60-minute set to the crowd and the mood of the morning. Our set lists typically include acoustic versions of songs by Fleetwood Mac, The Beatles, Tracy Chapman, Jack Johnson, Norah Jones, John Mayer, and a selection of current pop tunes reworked for acoustic guitar and vocal harmonies (for example, acoustic versions of songs by Ed Sheeran, Adele, and Taylor Swift). We also incorporate a few original songs that are mellow, melodic, and easy to listen to—these tend to go over well with regulars at the Welland and Pelham farmers’ markets. 

We are fully self-contained in terms of sound: we own a compact battery-capable PA system (Bose S1 Pro), two vocal mics, mic stands, and all required cables, so we can set up quickly and keep stage volume at a comfortable level for vendors and shoppers. We are very mindful of volume, especially in the morning and near produce vendors, and we frequently check in with nearby vendors during our first song or two to make sure the sound level is appropriate. We are also used to playing in a "background" context, keeping the energy warm and welcoming without overpowering conversations. 

In the past two summers, we have performed at the Fonthill Farmers’ Market, at the Welland Canal-side pop-up events, and at a series of Sunday brunches at a local café. Feedback from organizers has consistently highlighted our professionalism, punctuality, and ability to adapt to last-minute changes (for example, moving indoors due to rain or shifting our setup to accommodate vendor layout changes). We maintain an active social media presence (Instagram and Facebook) where we share short clips from performances, which we are happy to provide as demo links along with this application.

entertainer_information.genre_style.answer: Acoustic folk-pop, soft rock, and singer-songwriter covers with light originals
